codeReview.summary: The feature adds broad SSH-failure coverage around the shared live layers, but it does not actually validate the shipped CLI surface that the feature description requires. The MCP assertions are useful, yet the CLI half of the test suite only calls exported Effect helpers directly and therefore never checks real command rendering or exit-code behavior.

issues.description: The supposed CLI coverage never invokes the real CLI entrypoint; it imports and calls `runStatus`/`runPull`/`runRollback`/`runActivate`/`runDeactivate` directly (see also lines 37-43, 264, 322, 374, 429). That bypasses `apps/cli/src/cli.ts:409-444`, where the user-visible formatting and exit-code semantics live, so this feature does not verify the required behavior that 'CLI shows human-readable error'.

The test hard-codes the shared remote repo and active-dir paths (`~/repos/shuvbot-skills`, `~/.codex/skills`) and then exercises live pull/tool calls against them (for example the mixed-host `fleet_pull` case at lines 592-595). Mission guidance says existing skills repos are read-only for E2E work and write-path assertions should stay in isolated `/tmp/...` sandboxes, so this test adds avoidable shared-state coupling and flake risk.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The assigned `backend-worker` skill is package/TDD-oriented and does not tell workers implementing `tests/e2e` features to use the documented CLI/MCP validation surfaces. That mismatch likely contributed to this feature testing imported CLI helpers and an in-memory MCP server instead of the shipped user surfaces.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:28-67 instructs tests in `packages/<name>/test/` with `npm test -- --filter <package>`; the worker handoff at /home/shuv/.factory/missions/53d67669-5ba8-4745-9fd5-71906b40773d/handoffs/2026-03-16T02-42-35-267Z__e2e-error-propagation__cc264cd3-dfee-4376-91e4-9be8f6573625.json:115-119 explicitly notes the TDD guidance mismatch for this E2E task; .factory/library/user-testing.md:86 and :100 contain the CLI/MCP surface guidance the skill never references.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared-state guidance around remote sandboxes is currently inconsistent with the repository's E2E test patterns. Mission docs say existing remote skill repos are read-only and write-path assertions must stay in isolated sandboxes, but this feature (and other current E2E tests) still point at `~/repos/shuvbot-skills` and `~/.codex/skills` directly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/shuv/.factory/missions/53d67669-5ba8-4745-9fd5-71906b40773d/AGENTS.md:16 and :75 require isolated E2E test directories; /home/shuv/repos/codex-fleet/.factory/library/user-testing.md:81, :87, and :100 say never modify `~/repos/shuvbot-skills` or `~/.codex/skills`; tests/e2e/error-propagation.test.ts:83-86 still hard-code those shared paths.

summary: Fail. The new test exercises SSH failure behavior through shared live layers and MCP tool handlers, but it does not cover the real CLI entrypoint, so it misses the user-facing CLI error formatting and exit-code behavior promised by the feature. I also noted a shared-state mismatch between the documented sandbox rules and the repo's current E2E path usage.
